name: when-automating-github-actions-use-workflow-automation
description: Advanced GitHub Actions workflow automation with AI swarm coordination, intelligent CI/CD pipelines, and comprehensive repository management. Coordinates cicd-engineer, workflow-automation, tester, and security-auditor agents through mesh topology to create, optimize, and maintain GitHub Actions workflows. Handles workflow generation, performance optimization, security hardening, matrix testing strategies, and workflow debugging. Use when building CI/CD pipelines, optimizing existing workflows, or establishing automation standards.
capabilities: Generate CI/CD pipelines from scratch using 5 specialized AI agents
Optimize existing workflows for faster execution and lower costs
Debug failing workflows with systematic root cause analysis
Implement security hardening and secrets management best practices
Design matrix testing strategies across multiple OS and runtime versions
Create reusable workflows and composite actions for organization-wide reuse
author: DNYoussef
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing markdown documentation and prompt templates for AI agents. The static analyzer incorrectly flagged documentation examples as executable code. All 142 reported findings are false positives - there is no actual code execution, network access, or credential access in this skill package. Referenced scripts are external dependencies not bundled with the skill.
valueStatement: Creating and maintaining CI/CD pipelines manually is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill coordinates specialized AI agents to design, implement, optimize, and debug GitHub Actions workflows in parallel.
userTitle: Automate GitHub Actions workflows with AI agents
limitations: Requires external scripts (workflow-generator.sh, workflow-validator.sh) to be present
Depends on MCP tools from claude-flow package for agent orchestration
GitHub repository access and permissions required for actual workflow deployment
Cannot execute workflows locally without the act tool installed

useCases.title: Build CI/CD pipelines
useCases.description: Create production-ready GitHub Actions workflows with testing, security scanning, and deployment automation

useCases.title: Harden workflow security
useCases.description: Audit and strengthen GitHub Actions workflows against vulnerabilities, supply chain attacks, and secrets exposure

useCases.title: Debug failing workflows
useCases.description: Systematically diagnose and fix CI/CD failures including flaky tests, timeouts, and dependency issues

promptTemplates.title: Create new workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline for my Node.js project with testing, linting, and deployment to npm

promptTemplates.title: Optimize slow workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze my existing workflow at .github/workflows/ci.yml and optimize it for faster execution with better caching

promptTemplates.title: Debug failing build
promptTemplates.prompt: Debug my failing GitHub Actions workflow. The build job times out on ubuntu-latest with Node 18

promptTemplates.title: Implement matrix testing
promptTemplates.prompt: Add matrix testing to my workflow to test across ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest with Node 16, 18, 20

bestPractices: Always pin GitHub Actions to specific commit SHAs instead of tags like @v1 or @latest
Use minimal permissions (GITHUB_TOKEN) and scope permissions at job level for least privilege
Implement caching strategies for dependencies, build artifacts, and Docker layers to reduce execution time
antiPatterns: Using @latest or @main for action versions (security and reproducibility risk)
Granting excessive permissions to GITHUB_TOKEN beyond what jobs require
Running all jobs serially when independent jobs could execute in parallel

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through the claude-flow MCP integration.

faq.question: What are the rate limits for GitHub Actions API calls?
faq.answer: GitHub API has a 5000 requests per hour limit for authenticated users. Use GITHUB_TOKEN for authenticated requests.

faq.question: How do I integrate this with existing GitHub Actions workflows?
faq.answer: The skill analyzes your existing .github/workflows/ files and suggests optimizations or creates parallel workflows.

faq.question: Is my repository data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill only reads workflow files you explicitly provide. It does not access other repository data or secrets.

faq.question: Why is my workflow running slowly after optimization?
faq.answer: First runs may be slower due to cache misses. Subsequent runs should be faster. Check cache key specificity.

faq.question: How does this compare to GitHub's native workflow templates?
faq.answer: This skill provides AI-powered customization, multi-agent coordination, and advanced patterns beyond basic templates.
